description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof with visible wooden beams and a partially damaged, weathered appearance. The roof is covered with overlapping brown shingles, some of which are missing or broken, revealing the underlying structure. The exterior walls are constructed in a half-timbered style, with dark wooden framing and light plaster infill panels, reminiscent of traditional European architecture. The front facade includes a small, recessed entryway without a visible door, and the windows are set in irregular, asymmetrical patterns. The structure appears to be a single-story dwelling, compact in size, and likely built primarily from wood and plaster. The overall look suggests an old, rustic, or possibly abandoned residential building.
description: A rustic, half-timbered house with a steep, damaged gable roof and asymmetrical windows, featuring weathered wood and plaster walls.
description_view_0: Front view showing the recessed entryway, half-timbered facade, and damaged roof shingles.
description_view_1: Side/top view highlighting the overlapping brown shingles and visible roof damage.
description_view_2: Rear gable view displaying the wooden frame structure and intact plaster panels.
description_view_3: Opposite side/top view with a clearer look at the undamaged roof section and window arrangement.
attribute_entryway_design: Small recessed entry with no visible door, framed by timber beams and lacking decorative elements.
